description:
Location: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:293-310. Signature is fn split_path(path: &str) -> Result<(&str, String), SecretError>. The first element is borrowed from the input, the second is owned.

The asymmetry exists because the caller (line 72) wants String for label (to store in the struct) but only &str for slot (to parse as integer). Forcing a String allocation here saves a .to_string() at the call site, but the function signature reads as if there is a deeper reason.

Suggested rewrite: return (&str, &str) and let the caller call .to_string() once:

fn split_path(path: &str) -> Result<(&str, &str), SecretError> {
    path.split_once('/')
        .filter(|(s, l)| !s.is_empty() && !l.is_empty())
        .ok_or_else(|| SecretError::InvalidUri("pkcs11 URI path must be slot/label".into()))
}

This also collapses the body from 17 lines to 4 using split_once (added in Rust 1.52, well below the workspace MSRV of 1.75).

Tests at lines 638-671 will need a tiny adjustment (label is &str now).
title: split_path returns asymmetric (&str, String); use split_once instead
